data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 71,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.40% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 06, 2026, 08:32 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 71,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 71,005.679688 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.40% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Oil Prices Steady in European Trading Amid U.S. Decision on Russian Oil
data.description: Oil prices remained stable during early European trading after a significant rise of nearly 5% on Thursday. According to Jin10, analysts Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey from ING noted that the U.S. decision to permit the sale of Russian oil to India, with a lease term of 30 days, might slightly ease immediate supply pressures. However, they emphasized that this measure is not a game-changer. The exemption allows Russian crude oil and products loaded before March 5 to be sold to India, with the validity extending until April 4. They highlighted that this move is part of the U.S. government's strategy to curb the surge in oil prices.

data.title: Poland's Defense Minister Discusses Drone Systems and Middle East Situation with EU Official
data.description: Poland's Defense Minister held discussions today with the European Union's Commissioner for Defense and Space Affairs, focusing on the development of drone and anti-drone systems. According to Jin10, the meeting also covered the SAFE program and the current situation in the Middle East. The talks aimed to enhance cooperation and address security challenges in these areas.

data.title: UAE Criticizes U.S. Amid Ongoing Iranian Missile Attacks
data.description: The United Arab Emirates has expressed growing dissatisfaction with the United States as Iran continues its missile attacks on Gulf states. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing tensions in the region. The UAE's criticism comes amid ongoing security concerns and geopolitical instability, with Iran's actions exacerbating regional unrest. The situation has prompted calls for more decisive action from international powers to address the escalating conflict and ensure stability in the Gulf. The UAE's stance reflects broader regional apprehensions about the effectiveness of U.S. policies in curbing Iranian aggression.

data.title: Binance has resumed FLOW deposits, withdrawals, and listings; users affected by the security incident in December have been compensated.
data.description: According to the official blog, Binance and the Flow Foundation have reached a final solution regarding the Flow cybersecurity vulnerability that occurred on December 27, 2025. Binance stated that all affected users have received full compensation, and the platform has fully restored FLOW token deposits and withdrawals, with FLOW listings returning to normal. The Flow network is currently operating normally.

data.title: ECB's Escrivá Holds Off on Assessing U.S. Trade Threats Against Spain
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member José Luis Escrivá stated that he will refrain from evaluating potential U.S. threats to cut trade ties with Spain until any definitive decisions are made. According to Jin10, Escrivá emphasized the importance of waiting for clear outcomes before making any assessments regarding the situation. This cautious approach reflects the broader uncertainty in international trade relations, particularly between the U.S. and European nations. The potential trade disruptions could have significant implications for economic stability and growth in the region.

data.title: Etihad Airways Plans to Resume Flights to 70 Destinations
data.description: Etihad Airways is set to resume operations to approximately 70 destinations following a nearly week-long suspension. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the airline's efforts to restore its network after the temporary halt. The decision comes as part of Etihad's strategy to reconnect with key markets and enhance its global presence. The airline aims to gradually increase its flight frequency and expand its reach to meet growing travel demand. This move is expected to boost the aviation sector and support economic recovery efforts.

data.title: Iran Faces Major Internet Disruption for Over 144 Hours
data.description: Iran has been experiencing a significant internet disruption that has lasted for more than 144 hours. According to Jin10, data from the network monitoring organization NetBlocks indicates that internet connectivity within Iran is currently at approximately 1% of normal levels. This extensive outage has raised concerns about communication and information flow within the country.

data.title: iShares South Korea ETF Experiences Price Discrepancy
data.description:
On March 6, the iShares South Korea ETF (EWY) closed down 6.42% at $125.74, according to BlockBeats. During the U.S. stock market closure, the cash:EWY contract on Hyperliquid rebounded, trading at $131.1, which is over a 4.4% premium compared to the ETF's closing price.

With less than an hour before U.S. pre-market trading opens, it is typical for higher contract premiums to gradually converge with spot prices after the market opens.

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Dump 15,000+ BTC Since October
data.description:
Publicly traded Bitcoin miners have offloaded more than 15,000 BTC since October. The selling began after the market reached a peak and then experienced a sharp flash crash, forcing many miners to reassess their treasury strategies. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/03/06/bitcoin-miners-dump-15000-btc-since-october/

data.title: Pakistan Rules Out Talks with Afghanistan Amid Clashes
data.description: Pakistan has decided against engaging in negotiations with Afghanistan following recent border clashes. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the escalating tensions between the two nations. The clashes have intensified along the border, leading to strained relations and heightened security concerns. Pakistan's decision comes amid ongoing disputes over border management and security issues. The situation remains tense, with both countries maintaining a strong military presence in the region. Efforts to resolve the conflict through diplomatic channels have stalled, as Pakistan firmly opposes any talks under the current circumstances. The international community continues to monitor the situation closely, urging both sides to seek peaceful resolutions to avoid further escalation.

data.title: Maersk to Halt Container Services Amid Middle East Security Concerns
data.description: Maersk has announced the suspension of two container services in response to security risks in the Middle East and Gulf region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's decision to prioritize safety amid escalating tensions in these areas. The shipping giant's move reflects growing concerns over the stability and security of maritime operations in the region. Maersk's decision underscores the impact of geopolitical factors on global trade routes and logistics. The company is closely monitoring the situation and will adjust its operations as necessary to ensure the safety of its personnel and assets.

data.title: Bitcoin's Momentum Indicator Shows Signs of Recovery
data.description: Bitcoin's price momentum indicator is showing signs of recovery, according to crypto analytics firm Swissblock. The firm notes that the indicator is emerging from its most negative phase. According to NS3.AI, a sustained reading in the +0.50 range on this indicator would signal a full bull market. Swissblock highlights that past cycles suggest such positive phases can last for several months, indicating the potential for more than just a relief rally if the level holds.

data.title: Labor Statistics Bureau to Release Delayed Population Control Data
data.description:
The Labor Statistics Bureau is set to release new population control data that was delayed due to a 43-day government shutdown last year. According to Odaily, BNP Paribas Chief U.S. Economist Eggerhoff noted that the latest Census Bureau data indicates the Labor Statistics Bureau has been overestimating population growth since the end of 2024. The adjusted January data is expected to show a reduction of approximately 590,000 in the population aged 16 and above, a decrease of about 370,000 in the labor force, and similar adjustments in household survey employment figures.

Using experimental series that smooth past population control and immigration data, combined with the latest census data, the U.S. labor force is projected to grow by only 900,000 in 2025, with overall labor force growth in 2026 expected to be well below 500,000. The population control adjustments will only affect January's household survey data, meaning month-to-month comparisons of household employment, unemployment, and labor force indicators will not be directly comparable.

Bank of America Securities Economist Mishra stated that the good news is that key ratios, such as the unemployment rate and labor force participation rate, are typically minimally affected. Last year, population control adjustments increased these two ratios by 0.1 percentage points, while this year's adjustments may slightly decrease them.

data.title: Ford Recalls Nearly 890,000 Vehicles in the U.S.
data.description: Ford Motor Company has announced a recall of 889,950 vehicles in the United States due to safety concerns. According to Jin10, the recall affects several models and is part of the company's ongoing efforts to address potential issues that could compromise vehicle safety. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has been informed of the recall, and Ford is working to ensure that affected vehicles are serviced promptly. Owners of the recalled vehicles are advised to contact their local dealerships for further information and assistance. This recall highlights the importance of vehicle safety and the measures automakers must take to protect consumers.

data.title: Putin Prioritizes Military Spending Amid Economic Challenges
data.description: Vladimir Putin remains committed to increasing military expenditures, according to Lithuanian intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision comes despite Russia facing economic difficulties. The focus on military enhancement underscores the Russian government's strategic priorities, even as the nation's economic conditions continue to weaken. This approach reflects a broader trend of prioritizing defense capabilities over economic stability.

data.title: U.S. Non-Farm Payroll Report Anticipated Amid Middle East Tensions and Oil Price Surge
data.description:
PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter). The upcoming release of the U.S. non-farm payroll report is drawing attention as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have led to a halt in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, pushing Brent crude oil prices close to $90 per barrel. In response to these developments, U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a strong statement.

In related news, the U.S. government is considering imposing global export controls on AI chips, a move that has impacted the stock prices of companies like Nvidia. Meanwhile, JPMorgan Chase has announced a $20 billion investment in artificial intelligence, and Bitcoin mining companies are reportedly selling off BTC to invest in AI technologies.

Bitcoin's price has seen a correction, dropping from $74,000 to $71,000, as miners engage in significant sell-offs. Additionally, $2.3 billion in options are set to expire on March 6, leading to divided opinions in the market.

data.title: Nigeria Company Faces Challenges in Revival Plan Due to Missing Shareholder
data.description: A publicly traded company in Nigeria is facing difficulties in its revival plan as it struggles to locate its largest shareholder. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's efforts to move forward despite the setback. The missing shareholder has created uncertainty, impacting the company's strategic decisions and future prospects. The firm is actively seeking solutions to address the situation and ensure the continuation of its revival strategy. The absence of the shareholder has raised concerns among investors and stakeholders, who are closely monitoring the developments. The company remains committed to overcoming this challenge and is exploring various avenues to secure its growth and stability.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts High Valuation for Predict.fun Post-Launch
data.description: Polymarket forecasts a strong market performance for Predict.fun following its launch. According to Foresight News, the probability of Predict.fun's fully diluted valuation (FDV) exceeding $50 million within one day of launch is 89%. The likelihood of it surpassing $100 million is 72%, while the chance of it reaching $200 million stands at 44%.

data.title: Central Bank Plans Flexible Use of Monetary Policy Tools This Year
data.description: The central bank has announced plans to flexibly and efficiently utilize various monetary policy tools, including interest rate cuts and reserve requirement reductions, throughout the year. According to Jin10, these measures aim to support economic stability and growth amid changing global financial conditions. The bank emphasizes the importance of adapting its strategies to address current economic challenges and ensure a balanced approach to monetary policy. This announcement reflects the central bank's commitment to maintaining economic resilience and responding proactively to market dynamics.
